content:
13 min read
Just now

--

Blue-green deployment is not just a fancy DevOps strategy. It is what you start respecting after one bad release teaches you how fragile production really is.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Breaking production rarely starts with a dramatic mistake.
Most of the time, it starts with confidence.
A small change.
A clean pull request.
A green CI pipeline.
Then suddenly the login page is broken, the payment API is timing out, Slack is exploding, and everyone is asking the same painful question:
“Can we roll back?”
That is when you learn the truth.
Deployment is not only about shipping code.
Deployment is about surviving the moment your code meets reality.
The Deployment Looked Safe Until Real Users Touched It
The dangerous thing about most deployments is that they look safe from the developer’s side.
The build passes. Unit tests pass. Docker image builds successfully. The pipeline shows green check marks. The staging environment looks fine. Someone says, “This is a small change,” and that sentence has probably destroyed more…
